title: Scientific Method
transcript: The scientific method is a systematic process for acquiring knowledge. A scientist begins by making an observation, which leads to a question. A hypothesis — a testable prediction — is then formulated. Experiments are designed to test the hypothesis, and data is collected and analysed. Based on the results, the hypothesis is either supported or

title: Academic Integrity
transcript: Academic integrity is based on honesty, trust, fairness, respect, and responsibility. Universities expect students to acknowledge sources correctly, complete their own work, and avoid plagiarism. When these principles are followed, qualifications retain their value and learning becomes more meaningful.
